mission.title: wasmcloud: [RFC] Secrets in wasmCloud
mission.description: [RFC] Secrets in wasmCloud. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current wasmcloud deployment
steps.description:
Verify your wasmcloud version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
helm list -n wasmcloud 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working wasmcloud installation.

steps.title: Review wasmcloud configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant wasmcloud configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
kubectl get configmap -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=wasmcloud
```
### RFC Title

RFC: Secrets in wasmCloud

### Introduction

The goal of this RFC is to propose a new API specification for *secrets backends*, which are programs that will listen on NATS for requests for secrets and be able to satisfy them.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] Secrets in wasmCloud
steps.description:
## Feature or Problem

This commit adds a SQL database provider that supports the widely used, featureful Postgres database system.

One of Postgres's main benefits is a robust type system, and part of the work to make Postges possible to use from the wasmCloud ecosystem involves representing that robust type system in WIT -- see `bindings.rs` for more details.

This provider introduces multiple interfaces for making queries to Postgres cluster -- managed, named profile driven, and completely

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n wasmcloud -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmcloud
kubectl get events -n wasmcloud --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[RFC] Secrets in wasmCloud" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Feature or Problem

This commit adds a SQL database provider that supports the widely used, featureful Postgres database system.

One of Postgres's main benefits is a robust type system, and part of the work to make Postges possible to use from the wasmCloud ecosystem involves representing that robust type system in WIT -- see `bindings.rs` for more details.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with wasmcloud installed or the issue environment reproducible.
